element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic movement and projectile attack mechanics
Blue orb pickups grant additional firepower; maiden appears to explain/restore energy
Conditional appearances based on dragon state and firepower level in later areas
Maiden appears if firepower below three to restore energy
Dragon can take multiple hits before death; indicated by life bar
Required to progress; difficulty escalates through areas
Found in special orbs; grant temporary or lasting abilities
Dual weapon system mirrors Xevious: projectiles for air enemies, bombs for ground enemies
Using bombs on eggs yields blue or orange powerup orbs
Dying at checkpoint allows restart without full stage loss

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting blue orb for extra head (firepower increase)
Safe navigation through natural landscape sections
typical_tension_triggers: Dense enemy waves in mid-area sections
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty across six areas with conditional relief events; tension builds toward boss encounters, relief follows successful area completion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Eggs hidden in ground terrain require active bomb deployment to discover and unlock powerup orbs
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Special orbs (homing shot, multi-shot, shrink ability) found throughout stages; hidden level select accessible via sound test code

difficulty_curve.early_game: Maiden always appears in Area 1 to introduce rescue mechanic; safe introduction to firepower system and dual-weapon mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Areas 2-4 introduce conditional maiden appearances based on firepower state; random 1-up awards in Areas 3 and 5; difficulty escalates with denser enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Area 6 features maiden only if dragon has less than three heads; final boss Zawell requires mastery of all mechanics
notable_spikes: Boss encounters at end of each area
Area transitions where firepower carries over but enemy density increases
Final confrontation with Zawell in Area 6

notable_level_patterns.description: Areas move through distinct prehistoric/mythological landscapes (rivers, volcanoes, glaciers, jungles) with visually distinct enemy types and hazards
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Volcano area with lava hazards vs glacier area with ice mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Maiden appearances tied to specific dragon states (firepower level, head count) create variable progression paths on Blue Dragon route; consistent on Gold Dragon route
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area 2 maiden only appears if firepower below three

notable_level_patterns.description: Constant switching between air projectiles and ground bombs required; stage design presents mixed ground and air enemy encounters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All areas incorporate both enemy types requiring tool switching

notable_level_patterns.description: Midway checkpoints allow area persistence, reducing frustration from early mistakes while maintaining boss encounter difficulty

sources_used: tcrf - Dragon Spirit: The New Legend - The Cutting Room Floor (maiden appearance mechanics, Blue/Gold Dragon routes, regional differences)
wikipedia - Dragon Spirit (gameplay mechanics, dual weapon system, nine-area structure, boss progression)
